title: Hunter S. Thompson's "Red Shark" can be yours
author: Jason Weisberger
summary:
Hunter S. Thompson's personal "Red Shark," a red 1973 Chevrolet Caprice convertible gifted to him after he immortalized one in Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas, is heading to auction.

Christie's estimates the car could sell for $100,000 to $150,000. Thompson owned the Caprice from 1990 until his death in 2005 and named it after the "Great Red Shark," the drug-fueled road-trip machine driven off a rental lot and into infamy by Raoul Duke and Dr. 
